All the committee are homo this
morning except city Treasurer Eggle-
ston and City Clerk Fleming, who re
manned in Frisco to visit friends.


The Spokane Trades council at {.
meeting held last evening passed res
olutions endorsing the application Of
the Automatic Telephone company for
a franchise.


The resolutions reCite that the ex-
penditure of $500, 600 will greatly ben
eft the laboring man and they asls
the city council to grant the fran.
chise.


The International Tcamster's union.
Local No. 1853, have asked the city
council to grant the franchise asked
for by the Automatic Telephone com
pany, provided, however, that the new
company will pay the scale of wages
and adopt the eight hour day for
work performed as adopted by the
city.


The Building Laborers' Internal
tionll Protective union is heartily In
favor Of the franchise and asks the
council to grant Il.
